title: HOPPE Tokyo Aluminium Non Lockable Tilt and Turn Window Handle - 38mm Spindle - F1 Anodised Silver Finish
features: 1 x Hoppe Tokyo Tilt & Turn Handle Non Locking
Screw Hole Fixing Centres: 43mm, Spindle Width: 7mm, Spindle length: 38mm, Fixing Lugs Diameter: 10mm
Corrosion Resistance: 5 (Excellent), Country of Origin: Germany

Immerse yourself in the German engineering excellence embodied by this handle, featuring a Grade 2 rating and exceptional corrosion resistance. The HOPPE TOKOYO Duraplus handle is not just a window accessory; it's a testament to craftsmanship that stands up to the test of time.
description: Introducing the HOPPE TOKOYO Duraplus Aluminium Turn/Tilt Window Handle – a pinnacle of performance and style, meticulously designed to elevate your window experience. Finished in F1 Aluminium Silver Effect, this handle not only enhances the aesthetics of your windows but also delivers exceptional functionality. Key Features: Tested to Standards: Rigorously tested to DIN EN 13126-3: 23/180-0150/03/C1 and RAL-GZ 607/9, ensuring top-notch quality and reliability. Stop-in Position: Achieve optimal ventilation with the convenient 90° stop-in position. Cover: Boasting a partial cover cap for a sleek and modern appearance. Base: Crafted with a durable nylon base featuring supporting lugs for added stability. Spindle: Utilizes the robust HOPPE solid spindle for enduring performance. Technical Specifications: Product Type: Turn/Tilt Window Handle Version: Standard Rosette Shape: Rounded for a classic and elegant look. Material: Aluminium Finish: F1 Aluminium Silver Effect Spindle Size: 7 mm Spindle Length: 38 mm Lug Diameter: 10 mm Stop Position: 90° Additional Details: Country of Origin: Germany Corrosion Resistance: 5 (Excellent) Immerse yourself in the German engineering excellence embodied by this handle, featuring a Grade 2 rating and exceptional corrosion resistance. The HOPPE TOKOYO Duraplus handle is not just a window accessory; it's a testament to craftsmanship that stands up to the test of time.
